scope_boundary: synthetic runtime-adapter permission, approval, receipt, and rollback/residual fixture behavior only; no deployed adapter behavior, sandbox isolation, secret-handle safety, approval-service quality, rollback execution, real effect receipt validity, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has exact source mappings for the Runtime Adapters claim, public schemas for runtime adapter invocations and authority-use receipts, a synthetic runtime-adapter permission harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures, and proof adequacy follow-through that moves the chapter from executable-tests-needed to useful-but-too-narrow. Those artifacts support the design discipline around permission coverage, high-impact approval gates, approval expiry markers, effect receipt presence, rollback handles or irreversible residual capture, and authority receipt alignment. They still do not establish deployed adapter behavior, sandbox isolation, approval-service quality, secret-handle safety, rollback execution, real effect receipt validity, or runtime safety.
required_artifacts: deployed adapter traces across at least one real tool or runtime target
sandbox isolation tests with attempted forbidden effects
approval-service tests covering expiry, revocation, escalation, and denial receipts
secret-handle safety tests that avoid raw secret exposure in logs and artifacts
rollback execution tests for reversible effects and residual capture for irreversible effects
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
negative_results: runtime adapter permission fixtures are synthetic
schemas validate record shape rather than deployed adapter enforcement
deployed adapter traces, sandbox isolation tests, approval-service tests, secret-handle tests, and rollback execution tests are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: claim text interpreted as deployed adapter behavior or sandbox-safety evidence
effect receipt validity claimed without real effect trace
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record-gate behavior that passed, or after deployed adapter traces, sandbox tests, approval-service behavior, secret-handle safety, rollback execution, and real effect receipt validity are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove deployed adapter behavior
schemas validate record shape rather than permission enforcement
approval expiry markers in fixtures do not prove an approval service exists
rollback handles and residual fields in fixtures do not prove rollback execution
no independent runtime-security or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent runtime-security, formal-methods, or source-interpretation audit
non_claims: does not promote Runtime Adapters above argument
does not prove deployed adapter behavior
does not prove rollback execution or runtime safety
